entry.title: Washington Post: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Japan’s ruling party secures big election win in the aftermath of Abe’s killing
  • As Uber steamrolled into France, Emmanuel Macron was a ‘true ally’
  • Sri Lanka’s president and prime minister may be stepping down but chaos remains
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Strike on Chasiv Yar residences kills 15, traps dozens under rubble

  • Millions of Muslims celebrate Eid as pandemic restrictions ease
  • Gunmen kill 15, wound 9 in South Africa’s Soweto township
  • Family of American missing in Ukraine says U.S. response is inadequate
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • A Ukrainian priest splits Sundays between church and the front lines
  • As war nears 5th month, Blinken keeps Russian diplomats at arm’s length
  • Sri Lankan president to resign next week, says Parliament speaker, after protesters storm residence
  • Blinken criticizes China’s ‘alignment’ with Russia; divided G-20 ends
  • Japan’s youth baseball culture is brutal. Will Roki Sasaki change that?
  • Portugal battles wildfires amid drought, heat; 29 injured
  • 15 killed in Russian strike in Ukraine, 20 believed trapped
  • Israeli ministers to run as partners in upcoming elections
  • A year after protests, Cuba struggles to emerge from crisis

  • Israeli PM calls for Saudi relations ahead of Biden visit
  • Ukraine Muslims pray for victory, end of occupation
  • South Africa police say 15 killed in bar shooting in Soweto
  • Iran enriches to 20% with new centrifuges at fortified site
  • Bulls spare runners from gorings for 4th day in Pamplona
  • Yosemite wildfire doubles in size, threatens over 500 giant sequoias
  • Pregnant woman given HOV ticket argues fetus is passenger, post-Roe
  • Bannon says he will testify at public hearing as contempt trial looms
  • Two long weeks: Inside Biden’s struggle to respond to abortion ruling
  • About the Uber Files investigation
  • Takeaways from the Uber Files investigation
  • Response from Travis Kalanick to the Uber Files
  • Uber leveraged violent attacks against its drivers to pressure politicians
  • ‘Hit the kill switch’: Uber used covert tech to thwart government raids
  • Inflation Is Raging Because Globalization Is Fading
  • RIP to the LSAT? Let’s Kill the Bar Exam, Too
  • Putin’s New Weapon of Mass Disruption: Kazakh Oil
  • Billionaires Can’t Get Enough of Private Equity
  • The Post endorses Trone and Parrott in the Maryland 6th District primaries
  • Twitter’s case against India is crucial to the internet’s future
  • Biden has put the forced-birth crusaders on notice
  • Tax land, not buildings, to help with the housing crisis
  • A way around digital privacy concerns for women
  • A prayer for the dead
  • A Youngkin presidency would not change much
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 10 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-10 19:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 10 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-10 18:16PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong housing chief vows to locate more land for transitional homes after visit to subdivided flats and ‘cage homes’
  • Batches of Haagen-Dazs vanilla ice cream pulled from Hong Kong shelves for containing banned pesticide
  • Hong Kong residents crossing border at Shenzhen Bay Port can soon book Covid-19 tests online as eager travellers throng checkpoint
  • Hong Kong Science Museum’s dinosaur exhibition: what you need to know, from the ‘Big Eight’ skeletons to touching fossils and baby Toni
  • Mutual trust is key to success of ‘one country, two systems’ after President Xi Jinping’s promise to keep Hong Kong’s governing policy unchanged
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief says high-powered land supply, housing bodies to hold meetings this week
  • Hong Kong considers Covid health code system similar to mainland China, city logs 2,992 infections
  • A Hong Kong ‘eco-friendly’ toilet system aims to reduce greenhouse gas formation with natural sanitation
  • K-pop, J-pop or Mando-pop? No thanks, say young fans drawn to Hong Kong’s legendary Canto-pop stars
  • Five steps Hong Kong’s new leader can take to tackle income inequality
  • Visit by China’s top diplomat underscores importance of Zimbabwe ties
  • Relocation of UN’s COP15 biodiversity conference raises questions about its legacy for China
  • Xi Jinping and Joe Biden expected to talk within weeks: Antony Blinken
  • Head of China’s top court orders crackdown on ‘abhorrent’ crimes amid public outcry over attack on women in Tangshan
  • Shanghai to ramp up support for metaverse development, low-carbon projects, smart gadgets to help China’s economic recovery
  • China’s wind farms have a role in its renewable energy future, can they also ease effects of dust storms?
  • In Taiwan, Shinzo Abe’s death means loss of steadfast supporter
  • China steps up use of combat aircraft in Taiwan sorties as military tries to extend its reach
  • China pins hopes on local RISC-V start-ups to crack Western monopoly on CPU chips
  • US-China relations: how will Washington’s ‘forced labour’ law impact Xinjiang’s economy?
  • Hypersonic arms race set to change power dynamics between countries and the tempo of modern combat
  • Japan’s ruling LDP wins big in upper house election after former PM Abe’s death
  • Sri Lanka protesters refuse to leave president, PM residences until the men quit
  • How Indian royals from Rajasthan to Orissa are helping revive traditional arts and crafts
  • Asia faces a ‘rice crisis’ but China and India may escape the worst price rises, analysts say
  • Secretary of State Blinken vows pressure on Myanmar junta, urges China to do the same, during trip to Thailand
  • Sources: Abe shooter says he tried to make bomb; former colleagues shocked, called him ‘totally ordinary’
  • ‘Our Mekong is dying’: Locals reel from fish crisis as dams sprout up from Laos to China
  • China warns of increase in disease, pests in rice crops, potentially impacting production across Asia
  • Thailand has legalised cannabis. Now it needs to weed out the loopholes, critics say
  • Marcos must tread cautiously as US and China seek to reset ties with the Philippines
  • Japan exit polls in election two days after Abe’s assassination show ruling coalition projected to keep upper house majority
  • Novak Djokovic sees off Nick Kyrgios to win seventh Wimbledon title and 21st grand slam
  • Faroe Islands limits killing of dolphins to 500 a year after anger over annual hunt
  • South Africa gunmen kill 19 people ‘randomly’ in two bar shootings, say police
  • Russian rockets kill 15 in block of flats in Ukraine’s Donbas, say officials
  • Thousands march outside White House demanding Biden do more to defend US abortion rights
  • Ukraine war: Russian military destroys Western weapons amid eastern push
  • Moscow-born Elena Rybakina powers past Ons Jabeur to Wimbledon singles title
  • Techno party with Love Parade founder hits Berlin’s streets
  • As nuclear power makes a comeback, China is poised to be the net-zero hero
  • Macau to shut casinos for first time in two years as China’s gambling hub moves to curb rising number of new Covid-19 cases
  • Smallest flats see brisk demand at first Hong Kong home sale after Xi speech underlining desire for larger homes
  • To bridge divide at heart of US-China tensions, start with global rethink of public sector’s role
  • Carbon capture and storage tech too risky an investment in absence of quality data and low commercial readiness, report finds
  • Law governing cryptocurrency will boost Hong Kong’s leap into virtual asset market
  • Australia woos Hong Kong tourists to help restore pandemic-battered tourism industry, banks on pent-up demand
  • 5 Bali wellness experiences, from light therapy to breathwork, put to the test
  • Shenzhen in China becomes first mainland city to make ‘living wills’ into law covering end-of-life care
  • Alopecia areata, Jada Pinkett Smith’s hair-loss condition, now treatable by arthritis and Covid-19 drug, heralding ‘a new era’ for sufferers
  • Are Scientology founder L. Ron Hubbard’s far-fetched tales in China and Asia diaries actually true - at least partly?
  • Why ex-Lonely Planet publisher switched to counselling: Simon Westcott on death, and being abused by a teacher
  • High-end Spanish cooking that respects ingredients and tradition: Hong Kong moves on from molecular gastronomy of El Bulli’s Ferran Adrià
  • In Ukraine ‘everything is so patriotic now’, even fashion, as military T-shirt designs and hoodies with slogans soar in popularity
  • What can America, land of ‘zombie malls’, learn from Hong Kong’s shopping malls?
  • How Running Out of Time, starring Andy Lau and Lau Ching-wan, helped shape the ‘Johnnie To Cinematic Universe’
  • Sex or your phone – which would you rather go without? Expert relationship advice for those who said the former
  • Meet Alastair Wallace Stewart, Rod Stewart’s dashing son: he’s following in mum Penny Lancaster’s footsteps, modelling for Italian fashion brand Palm Angels – but he also loves swimming and football
  • Saint Seb! 5 times Sebastian Vettel proved he’s the nicest guy in Formula One: from campaigning for LGBT rights and supporting Ukraine, to reforming motorsports to combat climate change
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
